Reviews.Author: 2jx6pqqaad
Reviews.Comment:
I’ve seen a lot of big-name executors rise, fall, and fade out over the years | but Potassium has never let its community down. It genuinely feels like the refined, modern evolution of Synapse, and it consistently proves itself as one of the best executors available today.

Get it while you can; peak quality never lasts forever. All great projects eventually slow down, but right now Potassium is at the top of its game.

People who dismiss it without trying simply don’t understand what they’re missing. This is, without question, the most solid and reliable executor in the community. It started with almost no advertising, just pure quality | and that’s how every great platform begins.

Real > (https://imgur.com/a/F58WLwV)

Categories.Comment:
I’ve never run into any script-related issues while using Potassium.
Any problems I’ve encountered were on the developer’s side, not the executor itself.

Potassium provides a wide variety of execution environments, all listed on its official website.
You can view these in the documentation here: https://potassium.pro/

Potassium also delivers a 99% UNC / Success Rate when executing scripts, making it one of the most reliable options available.

I haven’t fully stress-tested this yet, but even during normal use my Task Manager shows extremely low CPU and RAM usage | often around 1% or even lower. That’s excellent performance, especially considering I’m running this on a high-end system.

CPU / Processor: AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D (8-Core)
RAM / Memory: 32 GB DDR5

Potassium has proven to be extremely dependable, and the statistics back it up. It holds a 100% sUNC rating, meaning it successfully passed every required function during its Numelon compatibility test (https://numelon.com/

The full test completed in 28.13 seconds, which is impressively fast for an executor and demonstrates strong internal optimization.

From personal experience, Potassium has always felt safe, stable, and straightforward to use | no unexpected issues, no reliability concerns.
[Image provided by the Weao Team.]

Potassium delivers a clean and familiar interface that keeps you focused on what matters most: scripting and elevating your experience. The classic Potassium layout includes organized tabs, clear line and column structure, and intuitive controls for executing, clearing, opening, and saving scripts.

Your script panel sits conveniently on the right side, and you can easily load your scripts each session through the built-in folder and executable system.

Potassium also includes a straightforward Settings panel, featuring both Editor customization and general app controls, allowing you to tailor the environment to your workflow.

A recently added Script Hub with search functionality makes finding and running scripts even easier.

One of the best parts is how smoothly the attaching process works | Potassium attaches live, without freezing or stopping your instance like many other executors do. It’s simple, responsive, and designed to keep you moving.

Potassium doesn’t push constant daily updates, but instead delivers consistent, meaningful improvements. Whether it’s bug fixes, hotfixes, or brand-new features, each update feels intentional and valuable. The owner frequently reworks and refines the API as well, ensuring Potassium stays modern and ahead of the curve.

It’s always exciting when a new update drops | you know something worthwhile is coming.

Reviews.Author: x0z9ah38g
Reviews.Comment: Executor isn't ass nor excellent. Just in the middle -  what really carries this executor is the fact its $20 for lifetime and how nobody is sane enough to compete with Potassium's price point yet.

Categories.Comment: Runs everything I throw at it, can't complain especially at this price point.

Before Hyperion updated it ran like a dream for many, but I was not one of those people I tried every fix and fix that I came up with, had the correct packages and prerequisites and still did not work. I had to do a full factory reset to get it working, afterwards it started working like a dream until Hyperion updated, it takes 140-200mb of ram most of the time.

They don't update fast and they don't communicate enough with their community, they have no chats in their discord server and post in announcements like once every couple months most of the time, ticket system is always full or  disabled. However, it inevitably does fix its issues despite being slow to do so.

UI is just standard, nothing special to it and looks nice and sleek but nothing that would blow me away. Very plain UI in terms of colour, its just black and white. Ease of us is fine, very self explanatory to use and all the buttons are located in familiar areas, and the saved scripts shown on the right is a perfect addition and takes the perfect amount of room.
Pretty much always the slowest to update, but the quality of updates are good they almost always fix the issues most users have.

Reviews.Author: 17qb2vj1g4
Reviews.Comment:
If you look past the poor customer support and the confusing server setup and focus on the price of the Lifetime plan, the Executor is really a great deal for everyone. There’s nothing else about it that could be considered a downside.
No, I'm not sugarcoating anything. But just because the Executor is affordable doesn't mean it's cheap.

Categories.Comment: Never had Problems with any Script. According to potassium: sUNC: 100% and UNC: 99% with rakNet library.
It does not use many resources. It is based on your hardware, as with any executor. Don't expect a high FPS with a GTX 1050 TI. It's not like your grandma would play 3D games.
There are no problems at any time. Just use the compatible build suggested by RDD (Roblox Deployment Downloader). No hassle.

The Discord server and user support are some of the worst I have seen in the Rblx exec scene. There are no tutorials or help until you see the people in the voice channels. Between Reseller, one guy advertised for a third party support server. That server is the only one that really helps. If you need help, here is the link: .gg/qXHwQajEER

The Executor's design is simple and nice.
Like in Reliability. No problems. Fast and new features. Nearly instant.
